title: Meta Removes AI Image Tool Following Privacy Concerns
subtitle: The company pulled its new Muse Image feature after users and professional unions criticized how it used personal photos without permission.

summary.en: Meta has officially ended its new Muse Image feature after significant public criticism. The tool allowed users to create AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. Many users were unhappy because they were signed up for the feature automatically. A major actors' union, SAG-AFTRA, led the pushback against the technology. Meta acknowledged that the tool did not meet user expectations regarding privacy.
summary.zh: Meta 在面臨重大公眾批評後，已正式終止其 Muse Image 新功能。該工具允許使用者利用公開 Instagram 帳號中的照片來創作人工智慧圖片。由於許多使用者在未經同意下被自動加入此功能，因而感到不滿。主要的演員工會 SAG-AFTRA 帶頭反對這項技術。Meta 承認該工具在隱私方面未達使用者預期。

paragraphs.en: Meta has officially removed a controversial new artificial intelligence (AI) feature just days after its release. The tool, known as Muse Image, was designed to let users create or change images using content from public Instagram accounts. However, the company decided to pull the feature after receiving widespread criticism from users and professional groups who were worried about their personal privacy.
paragraphs.zh: Meta 已正式移除一項備受爭議的新人工智慧功能，距離該功能發布僅僅數日。這項名為 Muse Image 的工具原先設計目的是讓使用者能利用公開 Instagram 帳號中的內容來創作或修改圖片。然而，在收到使用者與專業團體針對個人隱私疑慮所發出的廣泛批評後，該公司決定撤下此功能。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, the feature was part of a new AI model created by Meta Superintelligence Labs. It allowed people using the Meta AI chatbot to tag public accounts and use the photos found there to generate new images. The main problem, which caused immediate anger, was that Instagram users were signed up for this program automatically. This meant that anyone with a public profile could have their likeness—their personal appearance—used by others without their knowledge or permission.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，此功能是 Meta 超級智慧實驗室（Meta Superintelligence Labs）所開發的新人工智慧模型的一部分。它允許使用 Meta 人工智慧聊天機器人的民眾標記公開帳號，並利用在這些帳號中找到的照片來生成新圖片。引發立即憤怒的主要問題在於，Instagram 使用者是被自動加入此計畫的。這意味著任何擁有公開檔案的使用者，其肖像（即個人外貌）都可能在未經知情或許可的情況下被他人使用。

paragraphs.en: BBC News reported that Meta admitted the feature “missed the mark” and was no longer available. In a statement, the company explained that their original goal was to create a useful tool for creativity. They claimed they wanted to give people control over how their public content was used, but they ultimately listened to the negative feedback from the public.
paragraphs.zh: BBC 新聞報導指出，Meta 承認該功能「未達預期」，目前已無法使用。該公司在聲明中解釋，他們最初的目標是創造一個有助於發揮創意的工具。他們聲稱希望讓民眾能掌控其公開內容的使用方式，但最終他們選擇傾聽大眾的負面回饋。

paragraphs.en: The backlash was swift and strong. Many people took to social media to complain about the lack of choice. For example, the Emmy-winning actor Hannah Einbinder used her own Instagram account to criticize the tool. She pointed out that the feature was turned on by default and encouraged her followers to turn it off immediately. This type of public pressure highlights the growing tension between tech companies and the people who use their platforms.
paragraphs.zh: 反彈聲浪既迅速又強烈。許多人紛紛在社群媒體上抱怨缺乏選擇權。例如，曾獲艾美獎的演員漢娜·愛因賓德（Hannah Einbinder）便利用自己的 Instagram 帳號批評此工具。她指出該功能是預設開啟的，並呼籲追蹤者立即將其關閉。這類公眾壓力凸顯了科技公司與平台使用者之間日益緊張的關係。

paragraphs.en: One of the most powerful voices against the tool was SAG-AFTRA, a major union that represents actors and other media professionals. The union strongly criticized Meta’s decision to make the feature an automatic opt-in. They described the situation as an “utter miscalculation of public sentiment,” noting that the public is very aware of the dangers of having their digital images used without consent. A spokesperson for the union told The Guardian that they welcomed Meta’s decision to remove the tool, calling it the “responsible thing to do.”
paragraphs.zh: 反對該工具最強而有力的聲音之一來自美國演員工會暨美國廣播電視演員工會聯合會（SAG-AFTRA），這是一個代表演員及其他媒體專業人士的主要工會。該工會強烈批評 Meta 將此功能設為自動加入的決定。他們形容此情況為「對公眾情緒的徹底誤判」，並指出大眾對於數位影像在未經同意下被使用的風險已有高度警覺。工會發言人向《衛報》表示，他們樂見 Meta 移除該工具的決定，並稱這是「負責任的做法」。

paragraphs.en: Privacy experts also weighed in on the issue. The London-based charity Privacy International told the BBC that this situation is a clear sign that many AI companies view people’s personal data and images as nothing more than “raw material” to be used for their own gain. This perspective reflects a larger debate in the technology industry: how much control should companies have over the content that users share on their platforms?
paragraphs.zh: 隱私權專家也對此議題發表看法。總部位於倫敦的慈善組織「國際隱私權」（Privacy International）告訴 BBC，此情況清楚顯示許多人工智慧公司將民眾的個人資料與影像視為僅供自身獲利的「原料」。這種觀點反映了科技產業中一場更廣泛的辯論：企業對於使用者在平台上分享的內容，究竟應該擁有多少控制權？

paragraphs.en: This is not the first time Meta has faced questions about its AI plans. Before the removal of Muse Image, the company had announced that it was planning to bring similar AI features to other apps, including WhatsApp, Facebook, and Messenger. They are also currently working on an AI video tool. While these developments show that Meta is investing heavily in new technology, the recent U-turn suggests that the company will face more challenges if it does not prioritize user privacy and clear communication.
paragraphs.zh: 這並非 Meta 首次面臨關於其人工智慧計畫的質疑。在移除 Muse Image 之前，該公司曾宣布計畫將類似的人工智慧功能引入其他應用程式，包括 WhatsApp、Facebook 與 Messenger。他們目前也正在開發人工智慧影片工具。雖然這些發展顯示 Meta 正大力投資新技術，但最近的政策轉彎暗示，若該公司不優先考慮使用者隱私與明確溝通，未來將面臨更多挑戰。

paragraphs.en: For now, the removal of Muse Image serves as a reminder of the power of public opinion. As AI tools become more common in our daily lives, users are becoming more protective of their personal information. The incident shows that companies must be very careful when introducing new technology that uses personal data. If they do not provide clear choices and respect the rights of their users, they are likely to face similar resistance in the future. Meta has not provided any further comments on its next steps, but the message from the public remains clear: users want to be in control of their own digital identity.
paragraphs.zh: 目前而言，移除 Muse Image 的舉動提醒了大眾輿論的力量。隨著人工智慧工具在日常生活中變得更加普遍，使用者對於個人資訊的保護意識也日益增強。此事件顯示，企業在引入使用個人資料的新技術時必須非常謹慎。若他們無法提供明確的選擇並尊重使用者的權利，未來很可能會面臨類似的抵制。Meta 尚未針對後續步驟提供進一步評論，但大眾傳達的訊息十分明確：使用者希望掌控自己的數位身分。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To remove or withdraw something from circulation or availability.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（拉），這裡指撤銷功能。文中：However, the company decided to pull the feature after receiving widespread criticism from users and professional groups who were worried about their personal privacy.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To fail to achieve the intended result or to be incorrect.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 未達預期、失敗、沒抓到重點。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思為「沒射中靶心」，引申為失敗。文中：BBC News reported that Meta admitted the feature “missed the mark” and was no longer available.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To give an opinion or contribute to a discussion.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於拳擊比賽指「過磅」，這裡指表達看法。文中：Privacy experts also weighed in on the issue.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A complete change of policy or opinion.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 政策大轉彎、徹底改變立場。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（車輛迴轉），這裡指政策改變。文中：While these developments show that Meta is investing heavily in new technology, the recent U-turn suggests that the company will face more challenges if it does not prioritize user privacy and clear communication.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: Meta pulls new AI image feature after days of backlash

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Meta ditches Muse Image AI feature because it ‘misses the mark’ on users’ privacy

previousArticles.title: Meta Removes New AI Image Tool After Privacy Backlash
previousArticles.summaryEn: Meta has removed its new AI image feature, Muse Image, shortly after its launch. The tool allowed users to create images using public Instagram content. Many people and groups criticized the company for turning the feature on automatically. A major actors' union called the move a dangerous mistake regarding user privacy. Meta eventually decided to stop the feature, admitting it did not meet user expectations.

quiz.question: What was the specific mechanism that caused the most immediate anger among Instagram users regarding Muse Image?

options.A: The tool was only available to paid subscribers of Meta AI.
options.B: Users were automatically enrolled in the program without their explicit permission.
options.C: The AI tool could only access private accounts, not public ones.
options.D: Meta failed to provide any instructions on how to use the new image generator.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，造成民眾憤怒的主要原因是 Instagram 用戶被「自動」加入該計畫，這意味著公眾帳號持有者的影像可能在未經知情或同意的情況下被他人使用。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章未提及付費訂閱；選項 C 與原文矛盾，原文指出是針對「公開帳號」；選項 D 則非憤怒的主因，憤怒的核心在於隱私權的侵害而非教學不足。

quiz.question: Based on the reaction from SAG-AFTRA and other critics, what can be inferred about the current state of public sentiment toward AI development?

options.A: The public is generally indifferent to how their digital photos are utilized by tech giants.
options.B: Professional unions are primarily concerned with the artistic quality of AI-generated images.
options.C: There is a growing collective awareness and sensitivity regarding the unauthorized use of personal digital identity.
options.D: Most users believe that AI tools will eventually replace professional actors and media workers.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第五段，SAG-AFTRA 指出大眾對於個人數位影像在未經同意下被使用感到非常警覺，並稱 Meta 的做法是「對公眾情緒的誤判」。這顯示出大眾對隱私與數位身份保護的意識正在提升。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 B 錯誤，工會關注的是同意權與隱私，而非藝術品質；選項 D 雖然是科技界討論的議題，但並非本文推論出的主要公眾情緒重點。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what does the phrase 'missed the mark' mean in the context of Meta's statement?

options.A: Failed to achieve the intended result or standard.
options.B: Accidentally deleted a file from the database.
options.C: Targeted the wrong group of users for a test.
options.D: Refused to listen to the advice of privacy experts.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。片語「missed the mark」在文中用來描述 Meta 承認其功能設計未達預期目標或未符合大眾期待。選項 B 誤解了該片語的慣用語意，並非指刪除檔案；選項 C 雖提到目標群體，但該片語強調的是「失敗」的結果而非僅僅是選錯對象；選項 D 雖然 Meta 確實沒聽取專家意見，但「missed the mark」本身並非指拒絕建議，而是指產品表現不佳。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding Meta's experience with the Muse Image tool?

options.A: Meta plans to abandon all AI development due to the failure of Muse Image.
options.B: The removal of Muse Image proves that AI technology is too dangerous for public use.
options.C: Tech companies must prioritize user consent and clear communication to avoid public backlash when deploying AI.
options.D: Public opinion has little influence on the long-term strategies of large technology corporations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章最後幾段總結了此次事件的教訓，強調科技公司在引入使用個人資料的 AI 技術時，必須非常謹慎，並提供清晰的選擇權以尊重用戶權利。選項 A 錯誤，文中提到 Meta 仍在開發其他 AI 工具；選項 B 過度推論，文章並未否定 AI 的價值，僅強調隱私保護；選項 D 與文意完全相反，文章最後一段明確指出此事件提醒了大眾意見的力量。
